agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $100K legal services deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a legal services agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC for up to $100,000 covering general litigation and municipal advice. Rates: partners $375/hour, senior counsel $285-$355/hour, paralegals $170/hour. If approved, the agreement runs May 5, 2026 through December 31, 2027.

Legal Services Agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC for General Litigation and Municipal Advice
APPROVE a legal services agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC, for general litigation and municipal advice in an amount not to exceed $100,000, for a term of May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $500,000 legal services deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a new legal services agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG) for up to $500,000 to defend the city in tort/dangerous condition and personal injury litigation. BGG currently handles approximately 39 cases. The proposed term runs from May 5, 2026, through June 30, 2028. Rates: Partner $195/hr, Associate $170/hr, Paralegal $125/hr. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on $150,000 legal contract amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed fifth amendment to a legal services agreement with the law firm Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP. If approved, the amendment would add $150,000 to the contract, bringing the total to $400,000, and extend the agreement until December 31, 2027. The firm would continue to represent the city in municipal law matters, including personnel issues and lawsuits. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.

APPROVE a fifth amendment to the Legal Services Agreement with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP, in the amount of $150,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $400,000, and extend the term to December 31, 2027, to continue representing the City in municipal law matters, including personnel matters and litigation – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to add caste as protected category in municipal code
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would add caste as a protected category in the city's municipal code. If approved, this change would make caste-based discrimination illegal in Richmond in employment, housing, education, and access to public services. The city's Human Rights and Human Relations Commission would have the power to investigate complaints. This is a second reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced once before and is now up for final approval. The proposed bylaw would amend sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the Richmond Municipal Code.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision at the next meeting — this could affect your hiring, housing, and business practices if passed.
agenda_briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.d
Establishing a Commitment to Caste Equity and the Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination
ADOPT an ordinance (second reading) amending sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the Richmond Municipal Code to add caste as a protected category within the jurisdiction of the City’s Human Rights and Human Relations Commission – City Attorney’s Office (Shannon Moore 510-620-6505/Dallae Chin 510-620-5517/Brandi Robinson 510-620-5518).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 financial reports on investments, overtime, and taxes
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will receive March 2026 financial reports. Documentary transfer tax revenue was $1,225,231 for the month. General Fund overtime is at 77% of revised budget ($10.79M of $13.92M expended). The PARS OPEB account reported a 1.74% monthly gain ($1,081,101); the PARS Pension account gained 1.70% ($100,022); the CalPERS CERBT account had a quarterly gain of 1.43% ($203,237). The federal funds rate remains at 3.50–3.75%.

key_dates.label: Next FOMC meeting — federal funds rate decision

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $69,000 for Sister Cities delegations to China, Cuba, Palestine
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving $69,000 from the General Fund ($23,000 per city) to support the Sister Cities Commission's delegation activities with Zhoushan (China), Regla (Cuba), and Sebastia (Palestine). The Zhoushan trip is scheduled June 30–July 8, 2026, with 50 participants registered as of April 30, 2026. If approved, the city manager would be authorized to sign agreements with third-party administrators, and city staff would create a scholarship program for Richmond residents aged 16–45 who applied by April 30, requiring a minimum $500 participant contribution and a written post-trip report.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required at this time; the item has not yet been voted on.

key_dates.label: Sister Cities Commission funding decision — Richmond City Council
Zhoushan, China delegation trip dates
Zhoushan, China delegation trip ends

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.2.b
Appropriate Funding for Sister Cities Commission Partner City Delegations
APPROPRIATE $69,000 in General Fund to the Sister Cities Commission to support delegation activities with Zhoushan, China; Regla, Cuba; and Sebastia, Palestine; AUTHORIZE the city manager, with approval as to form by the City Attorney’s Office, to enter into agreement(s) with qualified third-party administrator(s) and take all necessary actions to administer appropriated funds in support of eligible costs associated with the Richmond–Zhoushan Sister City program; and AUTHORIZE city staff to develop and implement a scholarship program, in collaboration with the Zhoushan Sister City Commission, to support travel costs – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Nickie Mastay/Shasa Curl 510-620-6740).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $1.5M radio purchase from Motorola for Richmond Fire Dept.
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. to buy portable and mobile radios for the Richmond Fire Department. The proposed order would be for up to $1,500,000 and would run until May 6, 2029, with the option to extend for two additional one-year periods at up to $250,000 each. The purchase includes 50 portable radios for frontline personnel and 15 for Command Staff in FY 2026-27, plus mobile radios for two new fire engines. Funding includes $75,000 from the FY 2025-26 budget, with remaining costs covered by existing grants and pending congressional grants. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Purchase of Portable and Mobile Radios from Motorola Solutions, Inc.
APPROVE a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. to purchase portable and mobile radios in an amount not to exceed $1,500,000 for a term ending May 6, 2029, with the possibility of two one-year extension options in a not to exceed $250,000 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041/Den Mark Marcelo 510-307-8038).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to recognize May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proclamation that would officially recognize May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month in the City of Richmond. This is a symbolic declaration — it does not create new laws or require businesses to take any action. The proclamation is proposed by the Mayor's Office and is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting. If passed, it would show the city's support for mental health awareness and reduce stigma.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a
Proclamation Recognizing May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month
PROCLAMATION recognizing May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month in the City of Richmond - Mayor's Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to appoint Courtney Sanders to ECIA Transportation Board
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on appointing Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board. If approved, the term would run until July 25, 2029. The appointment is proposed by the Mayor's Office. This is a routine board appointment that does not directly change any rules or fees for businesses, but it could influence future transportation decisions that affect local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Appointment of Courtney Sanders to the ECIA Transportation Board
APPOINT Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board with a term that expires on July 25, 2029 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $540,000 police vehicle rental contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles. The contract would cost up to $540,000 from May 6, 2026, through May 5, 2029, with an option for two additional one-year extensions worth up to $360,000 more. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.

The proposed agreement is worth up to $7,200,000 and would cover Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an option to extend for three additional years (2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030). The total contract term would run from July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sole-source police mental health contract up to $525,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a sole-source contract (a contract awarded without competitive bidding) with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for mental health counseling and consultant services for the Richmond Police Department. The proposed three-year contract, starting July 1, 2026, would cost up to $525,000 total, with an annual base of $157,500 plus a $17,500 contingency, for an annual limit of $175,000. The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this contract will fund police mental health services for three years.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K community prosecutor deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office. If approved, the deal would spend up to $300,000 to assign a deputy district attorney (a type of prosecutor) specifically to work as Richmond's community-based prosecutor. This prosecutor would focus on local crime and safety issues in Richmond. The agreement would run from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this could affect local crime enforcement and business safety in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Proposed community prosecutor agreement start date
Proposed community prosecutor agreement end date

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.g
Agreement with Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office to Continue the Community-based Prosecution Program
APPROVE a five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office for the dedicated services of a deputy district attorney who would be specifically assigned as Richmond's Community-based Prosecutor, in an amount not to exceed $300,000, for the term commencing July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2029 - Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on $29,690 contract for Ferry Point Pump Station
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on approving a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler for $29,690.43 to complete engineering services for the Ferry Point Pump Station project, including raising a wall to screen equipment, installing gates and screening, roadway resurfacing, and trail improvements at Brickyard Cove Road and Dornan Drive. The city will also appropriate $45,000 from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund (account 40322931-400708-01U10) to pay final project costs. The contract runs from January 1, 2026 to January 1, 2028.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft FY 2026-27 operating budget
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will review the draft FY 2026-27 operating budget ($323.8M General Fund revenues) and FY 2026-31 Capital Improvement Plan ($173.2M total). The budget is balanced using $16.4M in vacancy savings (12% vacancy rate). Final adoption is scheduled for June 23, 2026. No new or expanded programs are proposed.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this budget could affect your business taxes and fees.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on opposing CO2 pipeline project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a resolution that would formally oppose the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve building a network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond to transport and dump carbon waste underground. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez has introduced the resolution, arguing the pipelines pose a risk to the health and safety of Richmond residents. The vote has not happened yet — this is a proposed decision that council will consider.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez at 510-620-6565 to share your views before the council vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on sidewalk vendor rule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed update to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code). The changes would update administrative procedures, waiver rules, and enforcement mechanisms for sidewalk vendors. This is a first reading of the ordinance — it has not been approved yet. If passed, it could affect how vendors get permits, how waivers are handled, and how rules are enforced.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your sidewalk vending licence or permit.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $390,000 e-bikeshare contract extension
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will vote on May 5, 2026, on a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility (Ride Today) adding $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed $3,540,341 to fund the e-bikeshare program through end of fiscal year. The program currently has 12 hubs (7 in TCC Project Area: Iron Triangle, Santa Fe, Coronado) and 4,560 registered users. Since launch, 4,664 trips were taken at a cost of $641.03 per trip. The program's long-term future will be decided during FY 2026-27 budget discussions.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on May 5, 2026 — if approved, the program continues temporarily; its long-term fate will be decided during FY 2026-27 budget talks.

key_dates.label: Council vote on e-bikeshare contract amendment

agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.3
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $100,000 legal services deal with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a legal services agreement with the law firm Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC. The agreement is for general litigation and municipal advice — meaning the firm will help the city with lawsuits and give legal guidance on city matters. The deal is for up to $100,000 and runs from May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027. The City Attorney's Office will manage the contract.

key_dates.label: Legal services agreement term starts
Legal services agreement term ends

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a Legal Services Agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC for General Litigation and Municipal Advice
APPROVE a legal services agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC, for general litigation and municipal advice in an amount not to exceed $100,000, for a term of May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $500,000 legal services deal with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a new legal services agreement with the law firm Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG) for up to $500,000. This agreement covers the cost of lawyers to represent the city in lawsuits and handle liability claims (claims where someone says the city is responsible for harm or damage). The agreement runs from May 5, 2026, through June 30, 2028.

key_dates.label: Legal services agreement with BGG takes effect
Legal services agreement with BGG expires

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b Legal Services Agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG) for Representation and Liability Claim
APPROVE a new legal services agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC, for litigation representation and liability claims in an amount not to exceed $500,000 with a term of May 5, 2026, through June 30, 2028 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $150,000 legal contract amendment for city litigation
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved a fifth amendment to the legal services agreement with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP. The amendment adds $150,000 to the contract, bringing the total to $400,000, and extends the term to December 31, 2027. The law firm will continue to represent the City in municipal law matters, including personnel issues and lawsuits. This decision is final and allows the City to keep using this law firm for ongoing and future legal cases.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c Legal Service Agreement Amendment No. 5 with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP (Burke) for Municipal Litigation Matters
APPROVE a fifth amendment to the Legal Services Agreement with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP, in the amount of $150,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $400,000, and extend the term to December 31, 2027, to continue representing the City in municipal law matters, including personnel matters and litigation – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved adding caste as a protected category in Richmond
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council voted to add caste as a protected category under the city's Human Rights and Human Relations Commission. This means it is now against the law in Richmond to discriminate against someone based on their caste. The change was made by adopting Ordinance No. 06-26 N.S., which amends sections of the Richmond Municipal Code.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your business's anti-discrimination policies and employee training to ensure they cover caste as a protected category.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.1.d Establishing a Commitment to Caste Equity and the Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination
ADOPT an ordinance (second reading) amending sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the Richmond Municipal Code to add caste as a protected category within the jurisdiction of the City’s Human Rights and Human Relations Commission – City Attorney’s Office (Shannon Moore 510-620-6505/Dallae Chin 510-620-5517/Brandi Robinson 510-620-5518).
Adopted Ordinance No. 06-26 N.S.

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received March 2026 financial reports on investments, overtime, and taxes
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received the City's Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for March 2026. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE', this item was for information only — no vote or decision was required or taken.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.2.a
Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for the month of March 2026.
RECEIVE the City’s Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for the month of March 2026 – Finance Department (Emily Combs       510-620-6740).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $69,000 for Sister Cities delegations to China, Cuba, Palestine
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council voted to take $69,000 from the General Fund and give it to the Sister Cities Commission. This money will pay for delegation trips to the city's partner cities: Zhoushan, China; Regla, Cuba; and Sebastia, Palestine. Council also authorized the city manager to sign agreements with outside administrators to handle the funds. In addition, council approved a new scholarship program, created with the Zhoushan Sister City Commission, to help cover travel costs for participants.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.2.b Appropriate Funding for Sister Cities Commission Partner City Delegations
APPROPRIATE $69,000 in General Fund to the Sister Cities Commission to support delegation activities with Zhoushan, China; Regla, Cuba; and Sebastia, Palestine; AUTHORIZE the city manager, with approval as to form by the City Attorney’s Office, to enter into agreement(s) with qualified third-party administrator(s) and take all necessary actions to administer appropriated funds in support of eligible costs associated with the Richmond–Zhoushan Sister City program; and AUTHORIZE city staff to develop and implement a scholarship program, in collaboration with the Zhoushan Sister City Commission, to support travel costs – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Nickie Mastay/Shasa Curl 510-620-6740).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $1.5M standing purchase order with Motorola for radios
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. to buy portable and mobile radios for the Fire Department. The order is for up to $1,500,000 and runs until May 6, 2029, with the option to extend for two additional one-year periods at up to $250,000 each. This decision is final and allows the Fire Department to purchase needed communication equipment over the next several years.

key_dates.label: Standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions ends

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a Purchase of Portable and Mobile Radios from Motorola Solutions, Inc.
APPROVE a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. to purchase portable and mobile radios in an amount not to exceed $1,500,000 for a term ending May 6, 2029, with the possibility of two one-year extension options in a not to exceed $250,000 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041/Den Mark Marcelo 510-307-8038).

minutes_briefs.title: Council proclaimed May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month in Richmond
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a proclamation recognizing May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month in the city. This is a symbolic declaration to raise awareness about mental health issues and reduce stigma. No new rules, fees, or requirements are created for businesses. The proclamation encourages community support for mental health initiatives and resources.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.4.a Proclamation Recognizing May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month
PROCLAMATION recognizing May 2026 as Mental Health Awareness Month in the City of Richmond - Mayor's Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

minutes_briefs.title: Council appointed Courtney Sanders to ECIA Transportation Board
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved the appointment of Courtney Sanders to fill a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board. Her term will expire on July 25, 2029. The appointment was made by the Mayor's Office.

key_dates.label: ECIA Transportation Board term expires

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b Appointment of Courtney Sanders to the ECIA Transportation Board
APPOINT Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board with a term that expires on July 25, 2029 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $540,000 rental car contract with Enterprise Holdings
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles. The contract is for up to $540,000 from May 6, 2026, through May 5, 2029. The contract also allows for two one-year extensions, which could add another $360,000 if both sides agree.

key_dates.label: Enterprise rental car contract start date
Enterprise rental car contract end date

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a Contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for Rental Car Services
AUTHORIZE and APPROVE a three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles for an amount not to exceed $540,000 for the period of May 6, 2026, through May 5, 2029, with two one-year, mutually agreed upon annual extensions for an additional total of $360,000 — Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received Richmond Police February 2026 crime report
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received the monthly crime report from the Richmond Police Department for February 2026. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE', this item was for information only — no vote or decision was required or taken. Council simply acknowledged the report as presented.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.b
Monthly Crime Report for February 2026
RECEIVE the monthly Crime Report from the Richmond Police Department for February 2026 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received Richmond Police March 2026 crime report
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received the monthly crime report from the Richmond Police Department for March 2026. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE', this item was for information only — no vote or decision was required or taken. Council simply acknowledged the report as presented.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.c
March 2026 Crime Report
RECEIVE the monthly Crime Report from the Richmond Police Department for March 2026 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

minutes_briefs.title: [NOT REACHED] Richmond council did not vote on $7.2M animal control contract
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond council was scheduled to vote on approving and authorizing a $7.2 million contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services for animal control services through 2030. However, the item was listed on the agenda but never reached a vote — likely because council ran out of time or the item was not called for discussion. No decision was made.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.d
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $90,000 contracts for police background investigators
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved contracts with T Jung Investigations and Steve Blanc Investigations to provide pre-employment background checks for police department applicants. Each contract is worth up to $90,000 over three years, from April 15, 2026 to April 15, 2029, with the option to extend for two additional one-year terms. The investigators will be hired on an as-needed basis.

key_dates.label: Background investigator contracts take effect
Background investigator contracts expire

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.e Contract with T Jung Investigations and Steve Blanc Investigations for Pre-Employment Background Investigators
APPROVE contracts with T Jung Investigations and, separately, Steve Blanc Investigations, to provide background investigations for Police Department applicants on an as-needed basis, in an amount not to exceed $90,000 per vendor over a three-year contract term of April 15, 2026, to April 15, 2029, with two one-year extension options – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $525,000 mental health counseling contract for police
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved a three-year, $525,000 sole-source contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for mental health counseling and consultant services for the Police Department. The contract runs from July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2029, with an annual cost not to exceed $175,000. The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions. A sole-source contract means the city chose this company without competitive bidding, which is allowed in certain situations.

key_dates.label: Mental health counseling contract takes effect
Mental health counseling contract expires

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.f Contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for Mental Health Counseling and Consultant Services
APPROVE a sole-source contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group, Inc. for mental health counseling and consultant services in an amount not to exceed $525,000, for a three-year term from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two optional one-year extensions, for an annual amount not to exceed $175,000 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802/Captain Matthew Stonebraker 510-621-1204).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $300,000 agreement for community-based prosecutor
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office, worth up to $300,000, to continue the Community-based Prosecution Program. This program assigns a dedicated deputy district attorney to work specifically in Richmond as the city's community-based prosecutor. The agreement runs from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029. The prosecutor will focus on local public safety issues and quality-of-life crimes in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Community-based prosecutor agreement takes effect
Community-based prosecutor agreement ends

minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.5.g Agreement with Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office to Continue the Community-based Prosecution Program
APPROVE a five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office for the dedicated services of a deputy district attorney who would be specifically assigned as Richmond's Community-based Prosecutor, in an amount not to exceed $300,000, for the term commencing July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2029 - Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $29,690 contract for Ferry Point Pump Station engineering
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for $29,690.43 to complete construction-related engineering services for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project. The contract runs until January 1, 2028. Council also approved $45,000 from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund to cover final project costs. A sole-source contract means the city hired this company directly without bidding it out to other firms.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a Contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project
APPROVE a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for $29,690.43 for a term ending on January 1, 2028, for completed construction-related engineering services and APPROPRIATE $45,000 from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project final expenditures – Public Works Department (Darcie DeLashmutt 510-672-3944/Tawfic Halaby 510-620-5482/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

minutes_briefs.title: Council received draft FY 2026-27 operating budget and capital plan
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council received the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. Council also reviewed the budget adoption schedule and received an update on departmental operations. This is the first step in the budget process — the draft budget is now available for review before final adoption.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the draft budget documents to understand how proposed spending and capital projects may affect your business operations or property taxes.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
O.1 Draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan
ACKNOWLEDGE receipt of the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and Fiscal Year 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan; REVIEW the budget adoption schedule; and RECEIVE an update on departmental operations, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Marina Point project with modified conditions
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council held a public hearing and voted to adopt a resolution that modifies the conditions of the Planning Commission's earlier approval for the Marina Point Residential Project. This means the project can move forward, but with some changes to the original conditions set by the Planning Commission. The decision is final.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
P.1 Appeal of Planning Commission Conditional Approval of Marina Point Residential Project
HOLD a public hearing to consider two appeals of the Planning Commission’s conditional approval of the Marina Point Residential Project (PLN23-117) and ADOPT a resolution Modifying the Conditions in response to the appeals and otherwise affirming the Planning Commission’s conditional approval - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

minutes_briefs.title: [NOT REACHED] Council did not vote on opposing CO2 pipeline project
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the agenda for a Richmond council meeting. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez proposed that the council receive a presentation on the Montezuma CO2 Hub and then adopt a resolution opposing CO2 pipeline projects in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties. However, the council did not vote on this item. Because the agenda verb was 'ADOPT' (a decision verb) and there is no mention of a deferral or the item being pulled from the consent calendar, the item was likely not reached before the meeting ended. No decision was made, and the resolution was not passed or rejected.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

minutes_briefs.title: [FIRST READING] Richmond council to introduce sidewalk vendor rule changes
minutes_briefs.summary: This item was on the agenda for a first reading only. Council was not expected to vote on the final ordinance at this meeting. The proposed changes to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (RMC Chapter 7.42) would update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms. Because this was a first reading, the item was introduced for discussion and will return for a future vote. No decision was made at this meeting.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477).

minutes_briefs.title: [NOT REACHED] Richmond council did not vote on $390,000 e-bikeshare contract extension
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond city council was scheduled to vote on approving a $390,000 contract amendment with Charleston Mobility to keep the citywide electric bikeshare program running through the end of the fiscal year. However, council did not vote on this item. The agenda shows it was a decision item (APPROVE and APPROPRIATE), but there is no deferral signal in the text. This means council likely ran out of time or the item was not reached during the meeting, so no decision was made.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Q.3
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).
